answer:
**In Charles Dunoyer's *Nouveau traité d'économie* Vol. I (1825), "climat" (climate/clime) serves as a secondary analytic category within his broader inquiry into the factors determining the distribution of *liberté* (liberty), defined as the perfected exercise and regulation of human faculties.**[^1] [^2] Dunoyer prioritizes *race* (Ch. 2) as the primary determinant of a people's capacity for liberty, arguing that racial varieties (e.g., Caucasian vs. Ethiopian/Mongolian) exhibit innate, unalterable differences in physical/intellectual faculties that persist across all climates, rendering climate unable to explain civilizational disparities or liberty's uneven global distribution.[^3] [^4] In Ch. 3, he treats climate as part of *circonstances extérieures* (external circumstances: soil, temperature, resources), which can facilitate or hinder faculty development—and thus liberty—but only secondarily to race/culture; he explicitly critiques Charles Comte's overemphasis on climate as near-deterministic, insisting internal human forces (energy, intelligence) prevail.[^5] [^6] Favorable climates (fertile/mild) may foster laziness in uncultivated races, stifling liberty, while harsh ones spur industry in superior races (e.g., Europe's progress despite poor soil/climate).[^7] No trace of "iqlim" (possibly an Arabic *iqlīm* for climatic zone, absent here).[^8]

**In Charles Comte's *Traité de législation* Vol. II (1835), "climat" is a central but ultimately limited analytic for human physical/intellectual/moral variations underpinning liberty's societal distribution.** Chapters 4–6 dissect climate as a hypothesized cause of racial *espèces/variétés* (species/varieties) and faculty differences affecting *perfectionnement* (perfection/liberty-enabling development).[^9] Comte refutes climate's role in producing immutable traits (color, features, stature), showing races preserve characteristics across latitudes (e.g., Negroes remain black/white-born in poles/tropics; Americans copper-toned from Tierra del Fuego to Arctic).[^10] [^11] He critiques Montesquieu's determinism (heat enervates, fosters despotism/slavery; cold invigorates virtue/liberty), noting definitional vagueness and factual contradictions (e.g., uniform traits defy temperature gradients).[^12] [^13] Climate influences *acquired development* within races (e.g., vigor, passions) but not innate capacities; liberty's distribution stems more from *moyens d'existence* (means of subsistence/places), species mixing, and institutions than climate alone.[^14] Like Dunoyer, no "iqlim."

**Interplay as liberty analytic:** Both use "climat" to explain liberty's unevenness (advanced in Europe/Caucasians; stagnant elsewhere) but reject climatic determinism: Dunoyer subordinates it to race; Comte to geography/subsistence/race-mixture. Climate modulates (e.g., harsh spurs Caucasian industry; mild indolence in inferiors) but cannot redistribute innate potentials. Corpus lacks "iqlim" or direct Vol. II liberty-climate link beyond faculties/morals; Dunoyer explicitly rebuts Comte's Vol. II/III climate weighting.[^15]

[^1]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL I*, Ch. 1 [full chapter](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/01-ce-que-lauteur-entend-par-le-mot-liberte/translation/).

[^2]: *Ibid.*, [p-f73842](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/02-i-influence-de-la-race-sur-la-liberte/translation/#p-f73842).

[^3]: *Ibid.*, Ch. 2 [full chapter](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/02-i-influence-de-la-race-sur-la-liberte/translation/), esp. [p-d3fec5](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/02-i-influence-de-la-race-sur-la-liberte/translation/#p-d3fec5), [p-ebe80b](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/02-i-influence-de-la-race-sur-la-liberte/translation/#p-ebe80b).

[^4]: *Ibid.*, [p-0027be](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/02-i-influence-de-la-race-sur-la-liberte/translation/#p-0027be).

[^5]: *Ibid.*, Ch. 3 [full chapter](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/03-ii-influence-des-circonstances-exterieures-sur-la-/translation/), [p-0446c9](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/03-ii-influence-des-circonstances-exterieures-sur-la-/translation/#p-0446c9) (quoting Comte).

[^6]: *Ibid.*, [p-405b54](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/03-ii-influence-des-circonstances-exterieures-sur-la-/translation/#p-405b54), [p-a405be](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/03-ii-influence-des-circonstances-exterieures-sur-la-/translation/#p-a405be).

[^7]: *Ibid.*, [p-b2bf21](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/03-ii-influence-des-circonstances-exterieures-sur-la-/translation/#p-b2bf21), [p-08c39c](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/03-ii-influence-des-circonstances-exterieures-sur-la-/translation/#p-08c39c).

[^8]: Corpus-wide search: 0 hits for "iqlim" (English/French variants).

[^9]: Charles Comte, *Traité de législation: VOL II*, Ch. 1 [full chapter](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/01-de-ce-qui-constitue-le-perfectionnement-et-la-degr/translation/).

[p-78b1b2](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/04-des-causes-physiques-auxquelles-a-ete-attribuee-la/translation/#p-78b1b2).

[^11]: *Ibid.*, Ch. 5 [full chapter](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/05-de-l-influence-attribuee-a-l-action-des-climats-su/translation/), [p-1c04c9](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/05-de-l-influence-attribuee-a-l-action-des-climats-su/translation/#p-1c04c9).

[^12]: *Ibid.*, Ch. 6 [full chapter](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/06-des-difficultes-que-presente-la-question-de-l-infl/translation/), [p-4fa21b](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/06-des-difficultes-que-presente-la-question-de-l-infl/translation/#p-4fa21b).

[^13]: *Ibid.*, [p-cf1894](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/06-des-difficultes-que-presente-la-question-de-l-infl/translation/#p-cf1894).

[^14]: *Ibid.*, [p-e820a5](/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/05-de-l-influence-attribuee-a-l-action-des-climats-su/translation/#p-e820a5).

[^15]: See Dunoyer Ch. 2 [p-d0df94](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-i-6da8ce/02-i-influence-de-la-race-sur-la-liberte/translation/#p-d0df94), referencing Comte Vol. III (contextually tied to Vol. II climate chapters).

args.query: climate liberty OR climat liberty OR iqlim
